name: Andrew White
abstract: Andrew White (September 6, 1942 – November 11, 2020) was an American jazz and R&B multi-instrumentalist (saxophone, oboe and bass guitar), musicologist and publisher.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to John Coltrane

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: So the Elvin Jones Jazz Machine has been stem rolling for several decades. ... At that time you had Andrew White, Ari Brown, Ryo? ... [INTERVIEWEE] Ryo Kawasaki? No. It was Marvin Horn, I think he was playing guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Andrew White was on this on oboe and Andrew is a story in himself because I know he played with Elvin’s group... [INTERVIEWEE] Well, that’s what some of us are (laughs)
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carter Benny
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve got a lot of them that was done by Andrew White in Washington [D.C.], and I marveled at them... I’ve looked at it, of course.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carter Benny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Weather Report, with Andrew White playing English horn... yourself on flute. I kind of remember that session.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: my first week of school Elvin Jones came through in a band he had with Andrew White and Ari Brown.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002
